fields.description:
## Problem / context
A subagent recently fast-forwarded the SHARED main checkout (/Users/joeoakhart/rebar) to satisfy a `rebar transition <id> in_progress closed`, clobbering uncommitted operator WIP. Root cause: the close's completion precheck reads the CLOSING checkout's git history and needs the merged `rebar-ticket:` commit reachable from HEAD, and our contributor guidance never told authors to fast-forward THEIR OWN WORKTREE (only) after a Gerrit merge.

Grounded mechanism (verified): `_referencing_commit_exists` runs `git log` over `repo_root` (src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:32-57), called at :153-161 with the "cannot confirm the work landed" error; the completion verifier is invoked with `ref="HEAD"` (:186).

## Scope
DOCUMENTATION ONLY. No `src/rebar` code changes; no new flag/parameter on transition/close. Edit only Markdown docs (AGENTS.md, CONTRIBUTING.md). Docs-only is the intended fix here (operator-set constraint) — the mechanism is correct; only the human/agent-facing guidance was missing.

## Plan
1. AGENTS.md — in the "Git workflow — land changes THROUGH GERRIT" section, near the close-discipline bullet, add a bullet: after a change MERGES and BEFORE the ticket close, `git fetch origin && git merge --ff-only origin/main` from inside the worktree; NEVER fast-forward/reset/stash the shared main checkout (it may hold operator WIP) — advance your worktree or a throwaway worktree at origin/main instead.
2. CONTRIBUTING.md — insert a NEW subsection §2f ("After merge — fast-forward your worktree, THEN close the ticket") immediately after the existing §2e (Submit); there is no pre-existing §2f. It carries the same two-part guidance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] AC1: AGENTS.md carries the post-merge fast-forward-your-worktree guidance AND the "never touch the shared main checkout" warning. Verify (both must hit): `grep -nF 'merge --ff-only origin/main' AGENTS.md` and `grep -niF 'shared main checkout' AGENTS.md`.
- [ ] AC2: CONTRIBUTING.md's landing recipe carries the same two-part guidance in a new §2f. Verify (all must hit): `grep -nF 'merge --ff-only origin/main' CONTRIBUTING.md`, `grep -niF 'shared main checkout' CONTRIBUTING.md`, and `grep -nF '2f.' CONTRIBUTING.md`.
- [ ] AC3: ZERO code changes — the diff touches only Markdown. Verify: `git diff --name-only origin/main HEAD` lists only `*.md` paths (no `src/rebar` path present).
- [ ] AC4: The pre-commit gate passes on the change. Verify: `make lint` exits 0.
- [ ] AC5 [operator-attested]: the change lands via Gerrit (`LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1`, then Submit/MERGED) and the ticket is closed from the FAST-FORWARDED worktree, without the shared main checkout being touched. Attestation evidence recorded on the ticket: the Gerrit change number, the MERGED status, and a comment confirming the worktree-only close.
